page: Amazon Web Services c3.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2680 v2 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this server provides 8 virtual CPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It features a 2.8 GHz clock speed, 25 MB of L3 cache, and supports Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX) and AES cryptography. The instance is configured with 15.0 GB of system memory, yielding 1.88 GB per core, and runs on the Xen hypervisor.

For local storage, the c3.2xlarge includes a bundled 160 GB NVMe SSD. Network connectivity is established with a 1 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmark results indicate average multi-core CPU performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 7237.82 and average memory bandwidth read-only speeds of 388.0 GB/sec for cached blocks and 50.08 GB/sec for uncached blocks. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 717.0.

Given its hardware profile, the c3.2xlarge offers a balanced compute-to-memory ratio suitable for legacy compute-intensive tasks, software compilation, and basic database operations. Its qualitative cost efficiency is defined by its older hardware generation, which provides dedicated vCPUs and local NVMe storage at a lower resource density compared to newer generations. It is best suited for static web serving, multi-threaded text processing, and lightweight database workloads that benefit from local SSD storage but do not require modern single-core performance or advanced instruction sets like AVX-512.
description: Amazon Web Services c3.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon E5-2680 v2 processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the Xen hypervisor, this server includes 15.0 GB of RAM, a bundled 160 GB NVMe SSD, and a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows average multi-core capabilities, including a Passmark CPU score of 7237.82 and average software compilation performance, though single-core metrics remain in the lower percentiles. Lacking modern instruction sets like AVX-512, the instance represents an older hardware class that offers dedicated compute resources and local SSD storage. It is best suited for legacy multi-threaded applications, static web serving, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c3.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 15 GB RAM, and a 160 GB NVMe SSD, delivering average multi-core performance for legacy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c3.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 15 GB RAM, and 160 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized virtual server featuring local NVMe SSD storage and balanced multi-core performance for legacy workloads.

15.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 160 GB NVMe SSD
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Xen hypervisor
Average multi-core performance for software compilation and database operations
Well-suited for legacy compute tasks, static web serving, and text processing
